question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R4 replaces its outbound `route-policy PASS out` toward R2 with a
newly-created `DENY` policy that drops everything. The iBGP session
stays Established (TCP is unaffected), but R4 advertises zero prefixes
to R2. PE-A's view of PE-B-attached customer networks goes dark.
Classic policy-change-fat-finger fault: the operator meant to filter
one prefix, accidentally denied all.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R4, the outbound route-policy PASS out toward R2 was replaced with a newly-created DENY policy that drops everything - a fat-finger policy change that denied all instead of one prefix.
Because the outbound policy filters every prefix before it is advertised, R4 propagates zero prefixes to R2 while the iBGP session itself remains up (the policy acts only on route advertisement, not on the session), so PE-A receives none of the PE-B-attached customer networks and cannot reach them.
Fix: restore route-policy PASS out on R4's R2 neighbor and remove the DENY policy.
expected_symptoms_summary:
BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.2.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R2, R4 (2 instances):
- R2: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
- R4: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
route-policy DENY
  drop
end-policy
!
router bgp 100
neighbor 192.0.2.2
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-policy DENY out

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router bgp 100
neighbor 192.0.2.2
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  route-policy PASS out
!
no route-policy DENY

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:16:55.898 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.4 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:16:55.896 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE : neighbor 192.0.2.2 Up (VRF: default) (AS: 100)
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:16:55.898 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:16:55.896 UTC: bgp[1084]: %ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE : Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
